pr.title: Remove failing CI badges for now
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Removed two failing GitHub Actions badges from .github/README.md: publish-packages and publish-pages. The PR body says this was temporary while waiting on #1085 and #1102, and notes publish-packages has a similar problem to #1102 but likely needs a rewrite.
what_changed: Deleted the publish-packages workflow badge from .github/README.md.
Deleted the publish-pages workflow badge from .github/README.md.
Left the build-melee badge, Code Progress badge, Data Progress badge, and GC/Wii Decompilation Discord badge intact.
smart_moves: Avoided advertising known-failing CI workflows in the repository README while the underlying workflow issues were unresolved.
Kept the change minimal and reversible: only two badge lines were removed, with no workflow or code changes.
Documented the temporary rationale in the PR body by linking the removal to pending work in #1085 and #1102.
decomp_lessons: For project-facing documentation, distinguish core build/status signals from auxiliary automation signals; failing auxiliary badges can create noise even when decompilation work itself is unaffected.
When CI workflows are known broken and awaiting larger fixes, temporarily removing README badges can reduce misleading status indicators without altering build behavior.
Small documentation maintenance PRs can be useful for keeping contributor-facing project state accurate during infrastructure transitions.
naming_conventions: Workflow badge labels matched GitHub Actions workflow names: build-melee, publish-packages, and publish-pages.
Progress badges used explicit labels, Code Progress and Data Progress, backed by progress.decomp.club endpoints for GALE01 dol code/data measurements.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states: waiting on #1085 and #1102; publish-packages has a similar problem to #1102, but likely needs to be rewritten.
searchable_terms: Remove failing CI badges for now
follow_up_queries: Check PR #1085 for the dependency mentioned in the PR body.
Check PR #1102 for the related publish workflow problem.
Look for later PRs that restore publish-packages or publish-pages badges after workflow fixes.
Search for publish-packages workflow rewrites or publish-pages workflow repairs.
